description: Outside a multi-story building, cars drive along the street as upbeat instrumental music plays.

The setting changes to an apartment living room where Joey stands near a large white ceramic greyhound statue, facing Rachel, Phoebe, Ross, and Monica. The room features a red leather sofa and eclectic decor.
Joey spreads his arms slightly and says, "So, what do you think? Casa de Joey." The group looks around the room.
The group continues to silently survey the living room, their facial expressions neutral.
Resting his hands on his hips, Joey smiles and says, "I decorated it myself." Monica immediately replies, "Get out."
Joey laughs and nods. Rachel steps forward toward a tall, framed metallic object.
Rachel examines the object and says, "Wow, Joey, this is art." Joey places his hands on his hips and replies, "Art it is."
Ross crouches beside a glass-topped coffee table with a black panther statue as its base.
Kneeling, Ross touches the table's edge and says, "Well, check this out. Is it a coffee table? Is it a panther? There's no need to decide."
Rachel sits on the red leather sofa and picks up a fuzzy magenta pillow.
Patting the pillow in her lap, Rachel says, "Hey, nice pillow."
Continuing to stroke the fuzzy material, Rachel asks, "So now tell me, is this genuine muppet skin?" Joey smiles in response.
Phoebe gestures toward a rectangular water feature with water running down a textured surface.
Pointing at the water feature, Phoebe says, "Hey, excellent. Excellent water table thing."
Joey gestures toward the water feature and says, "Thanks. Yeah, I love this." He turns his head toward the group and adds, "But you know what makes me want to pee?"
Joey holds his hands in front of his waist, looking at Phoebe.
Phoebe nods and says, "Yeah, well, me too." Joey gestures toward her and replies, "Yeah, I think that's the challenge."
Rachel turns her head toward Joey.
Still holding the pillow, Rachel asks, "Hey, how come Chandler didn't come?"
Ross is now sitting in a red, curved armchair, leaning back.
Gesturing slightly with his hand, Ross answers, "It's because he had a thing with the thing."
Joey nods slowly, his gaze lowered slightly.
Nodding, Joey says, "Right. I got it."
Phoebe turns toward Joey, bringing her hands together.
Looking at Joey, Phoebe says, "So why don't you show us the rest of your casa."
Joey replies, "Yeah."
Joey turns his body slightly.
Joey waves his arm and says, "Oh, the best part. Come on." He turns and begins walking toward a hallway, followed by the others.
Joey walks through the hallway, opens a door, and steps inside, holding the door open as Monica and Rachel approach.
Joey says, "Yeah." as Monica and Rachel step into the doorway.
Joey stands in a bathroom with a black toilet and a painting of a green dog on the wall. Monica and Rachel stand in the doorway.
Joey raises his hand toward the space behind the door and says, "Hey,"
Joey points a finger upward.
Looking down at the black toilet, Monica says, "Nice toilet."
Joey points his finger toward the wall behind Monica.
Joey gestures behind Monica and says, "No, behind it." Monica turns, looks, and says, "Well, you have a phone in here." Joey points at her and says, "That's right."
Joey leans one arm casually against the doorframe, smiles at Monica, and says, "I have a phone in here."
Monica smiles back, maintaining eye contact with Joey.
Monica's smile fades as she says, "Joey, promise me something."
Joey asks, "Yeah?" Monica replies, "Never call me from that phone."
Joey looks at Monica. The screen fades to black and the audio drops to silence.
The screen remains completely black with no sound.
The scene changes to a busy coffee shop with ambient chatter. Ross, Chandler, and Monica sit on a large orange couch. Rachel, wearing a waitress apron, walks behind the couch carrying a tray.
Rachel hands a mug to a man at the counter, then turns back toward the couch.
Rachel says, "Okay."
She bends forward and sets a cup down on the table, adding, "Here we go."
Rachel stands upright, holding the tray at her side.
Standing near Chandler, Rachel says, "Honey, I'm sorry. They were all out of apple pie." On-screen text reads: "SUPERVISING PRODUCER MICHAEL BORKOW".
Rachel points toward the counter and says, "Someone just got the last piece."
Rachel lowers her hand and turns slightly as quick footsteps approach.

Phoebe rushes into the shop, her volume elevated as she repeatedly says, "My God." She stops near the couch, holding a small card. On-screen text reads: "PRODUCED BY TODD STEVENS". She says loudly, "You are not going to believe this. I have just been discovered."
Chandler shifts his posture on the couch.
Chandler leans his head back on the couch and says, "Now, wait a minute."
Gesturing with one hand, Chandler continues, "I claimed you in the name of France four years ago."
Phoebe smiles widely, lightly bouncing and shaking her head, holding the card in her hands. On-screen text reads: "PRODUCER BETSY BORNS".

Phoebe gestures emphatically with her hands and says, "Anyway, okay? Now promise you won't freak out and say how great this is until I'm done. Okay. Okay." The group nods. On-screen text reads: "WRITTEN BY ADAM CHASE".
Phoebe takes a breath, maintaining her wide smile.
Phoebe holds up the card and says rapidly, "I just met this producer of this teeny record company who said that I have a very fresh offbeat sound and she wants to do a"
She finishes enthusiastically, "demo of Smelly Cat." Her friends on the couch immediately react with wide smiles and open mouths.
The friends cheer and applaud. Phoebe points at them, repeatedly gesturing with her hands for them to stop.
Phoebe says, "I told you not to do that yet," shaking her hands. She pauses, then adds, "Okay, and she wants to do a video."
The group on the couch gasps and begins to cheer again.
Phoebe quickly raises her hands and says loudly, "I'm not done yet, okay?" On-screen text reads: "DIRECTED BY MICHAEL LEMBECK".
Phoebe lowers her hands slightly and inhales.
She brings her hands to her chest and says, "God. Okay, if that goes well, they may even want to make an album."
The group on the couch stares at her in stunned silence. Monica raises a hand slightly.
Phoebe lowers her hands, smiles, and says, "I'm done now."
The group bursts into loud cheers, and Phoebe jumps up and down with excitement.
In a coffee shop, Monica and Chandler sit on an orange couch. Rachel sits at the counter, resting her forehead in her hand, with Ross standing beside her. Rachel says, "Oh, God, Ross."

Rachel lowers her hand and says, "Okay, if you care about me at all you'll get the pie out of the man's hood." Ross leans in and asks, "Get the what?" Rachel replies, "The pie in the hood. Pie in the hood."
Rachel points and says, "Go." Ross turns and walks toward a man in a green coat standing nearby.
Ross reaches into the hood of the man's coat and pulls out a mashed piece of pie.
The man turns around to face Ross, who stands still while holding the pie in his hands.
The man looks at Ross and asks, "What are you doing?"
Ross holds up the pie and says, "I'm sorry. My pie was was in your hood."
The man stares at Ross, then turns and walks toward the exit. Ross wipes the pie off his hands with a napkin.
Ross turns to Rachel and says, "Now I just have to get the coffee out of that guy's pants and I'll be back in the hospital by seven."
Ross walks away, and Rachel rests her face in her hand. The setting changes to an apartment hallway.
Monica, wearing a red robe, stands outside a closed door and knocks on it repeatedly.
Rachel walks into the hallway carrying a laundry basket as Monica says, "Damn it, Ross. Get your butt out of the bathroom."
Monica lowers her hands and shifts her weight in front of the door.
Ross's muffled voice says from behind the door, "Calm down. I'm blow drying."
Monica turns her head sharply toward the door.
Monica yells at the door, "Blow drying what? You have no hair."
Rachel walks into the kitchen and sets the laundry basket on a table.
Rachel starts folding clothes and asks, "What's going on?" Monica points toward the hallway and replies, "Your boyfriend has been in there for over an hour."
Monica gestures with her hands as she says, "Can't believe it's. Like I'm living with him again. He's here when I go to sleep. He's here when I wake up. He's"
Monica raises her hands and continues, "here when I want to use the shower."
Monica crosses her arms and says, "Oh, feel like I'm 16 all over again."
Rachel continues folding clothes and says, "Well, you're not 16. You're both adults now." Monica turns back to the door, knocks on it, and shouts, "get out. You do. But"
Rachel pulls a pair of pants from the basket and smooths them out.
Rachel holds up a shirt and says, "or he's rubber in your glue."
The bathroom door opens, and Ross steps out, running his hand through his hair. He walks into the living area.
Ross gestures toward the bathroom and says, "All yours."
Monica stares fixedly at Ross.
Monica walks toward the bathroom saying, "I hope you cleaned your hair out of the drain." Ross mimics her in a high-pitched voice, saying, "I hope you clean your hair"
Monica stops in the doorway, turns back to Ross, and tightens her lips.
Monica says, "shut up, Shamoo."
Monica continues to stare at Ross.
Monica yells, "Cut it out," as she steps toward the bathroom.
Ross mimics her again in a high-pitched voice, saying "Me. Me." as he walks toward Rachel.
Monica walks into the bathroom and shuts the door. Ross smiles and approaches Rachel at the table.
Rachel stops folding clothes, looks at Ross, and says, "I've never wanted you more."
Ross mutters indistinctly and distorts his face before turning away from Rachel.
Ross walks out of the room. The setting changes to a modern apartment with bright red seating and a large white ceramic greyhound. Joey stands with his hands in his pockets.
Joey looks slowly around the room, his gaze resting on the white ceramic dog.
He tilts his head slightly as he continues to stare at the ceramic dog.
Joey walks slowly around the ceramic dog and looks down at the floor.
He slowly walks toward the front door, stopping near a tall, dark decorative piece attached to the wall.
He turns toward the wall intercom by the door.
Joey presses a button on the intercom panel, and a buzzer sounds over the speaker.
A muffled voice from the intercom answers. Joey says, "Louie."
Joey asks, "Where's Howard?"
The voice from the intercom replies, "He went home."
Joey nods slightly.
Joey says, "He said he might stop up after his shift."
The intercom voice responds, "He said to tell you he wasn't feeling so good."
Joey rests his head against the wall next to the intercom.
Joey presses the button again and asks, "Hey, what time do you get off?"
A brief pause passes before the intercom responds.
The intercom voice replies, "I'm not feeling too good either."
Joey lowers his hand from the intercom. The setting changes to an apartment kitchen, focusing on two plush dog-shaped slippers resting on a counter.
Chandler sits cross-legged on the counter behind the slippers, leaning forward with his elbows on his knees, gazing down at them.
Chandler looks down at the slippers and says, "So, what do you say, boys? Should I call them?"
Chandler reaches forward and pinches the nose of the left slipper. A mechanical barking sound emits from it.
He pinches the nose of the right slipper, which emits the same mechanical barking sound.
Chandler nods slightly, looking at the slippers, and says, "Well, you know what they say."
Chandler continues, "Ask your slippers a question,"
He stares forward without moving.
Chandler adds, "you're going crazy."
The setting changes back to Joey's apartment. Joey stands by a tall, colorful glass sculpture, adjusting one of its wavy pieces.
He places his hands on his hips and looks at the glass sculpture.
Joey straddles the white ceramic greyhound and scoots it forward across the carpet while holding a long stick, making a rattling noise.
Joey stops moving the greyhound and says, "Freaking art."
In a kitchen, Chandler holds a large portable phone to his ear. "Hello? Hey," he says.
Chandler gestures with his free hand and says, "listen, I'm sorry I didn't make it over there today."
The setting changes to Joey's apartment. Joey holds a portable phone to his ear, pacing near a red armchair. "Oh, that's okay," he says.
Joey nods slightly and adds, "You had a thing."

The setting changes back to Chandler in his kitchen. Chandler says, "Yeah, well, I hear the place looks great." The setting returns to Joey, who gestures dismissively and says, "Oh, forget about it. I'm having a ball."
Chandler walks through his apartment holding the phone and asks, "How's the apartment doing?"
Chandler continues walking and gestures with his hand. "Oh, hey, it's terrific. I mean, it's a regular space"
Chandler puts a hand on his hip.
"fest," Chandler adds.
Joey holds the phone to his ear and looks downward.
"Well, great," Joey says.
Chandler rubs his fingers together and says, "Yeah, I just wanted to call and say hey."
Joey looks up and says, "Well, okay then."
A rhythmic beeping sound begins. Chandler asks, "Was that the oven timer?" The scene shifts to Joey, who picks up a television remote. "That's right, my friend," Joey replies.

Joey points the remote forward, sits on the red couch, and says, "It's time for Baywatch." A television screen turns on, showing the "Baywatch" title over a water background, accompanied by energetic instrumental music.
Chandler reclines on his couch with his feet resting on a coffee table. He watches the television screen.

Joey leans forward on his couch and asks, "Can you believe they gave Stephanie skin cancer?" The scene shifts to Chandler on his couch, who replies, "I still can't believe they promoted her to lieutenant."
Joey watches the television, keeping the phone to his ear.
Joey says, "You're just saying that because you're in love with Yasmine Bleeth."
Chandler gestures outward with his hand and says, "Well, how could anyone not be in love with Yasmine Bleeth?"
The television shows three characters in red swimsuits running on a beach. Joey sits up quickly.
"Hey, they're running," Joey says, kicking his legs rapidly in the air.
The television shows a close-up of a blonde woman running. Chandler gestures forward and says, "See, this is the brilliance of the show."
"I say," Chandler adds.
"Always keep them running," Joey says.
The television shows another woman in a red swimsuit running.
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "All the time running."
The television shows two women running. Chandler pumps his fist in the air.
Chandler pumps his fist again and shouts, "Run, Yasmine, run like the wind."
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Phoebe, Joey, and Monica sit together on a sofa.
Phoebe holds a blue mug and asks, "But I thought you wanted to live by yourself." Joey replies, "I did."
Joey gestures with his hands and adds, "I thought it'd be great."
Joey continues, "I figured I'd have, like, time alone with my thoughts."
Joey shrugs slightly and says, "But, you know, turns out I don't have as many thoughts as you'd think."
Phoebe keeps the mug near her chest and looks toward Joey.
Phoebe asks, "Joey, why don't you talk to Chandler about moving back?"
Joey asks, "You really think he'd take me?"
Joey explains, "I mean, we had a pretty good talk last night. But when I moved out, I hurt him bad." Monica places a hand on Joey's arm and says, "I promise you, he would definitely want"

"you back," Monica finishes. The setting changes to Chandler's apartment. Ross sits in an armchair and says, "I'm telling you, there's no way he's moving back." Chandler, sitting on the couch beside Rachel, replies, "Well, we had one of the greatest talks we ever had last night."
Chandler continues, "I mean, it was like when we first started living together." Rachel rubs Chandler's shoulder.
Ross leans forward in his chair.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Look, I know you don't want to hear this right now but we've seen him in his new place, all right? And he's happy."
Ross keeps his gaze focused on Chandler.
"He's decorated," Ross adds.
Rachel keeps her hand on Chandler's shoulder and looks at him.
Rachel says, "Look, Chandler, he has moved on, okay? You have to, too."
Ross gestures forward and says, "But no, you're just going to have to accept the fact that you're just friends now, okay? You're not"
"roommates anymore," Ross finishes. Rachel wraps her arms around Chandler.
The setting changes to a recording studio.
A man wearing a green shirt and a cap stands near a microphone. "Hey, Phoebe," he says over the studio speaker.
"Ready to try one?" the man asks.
Phoebe, wearing headphones and holding a red acoustic guitar, smiles and replies, "Okay."
Phoebe adjusts her position and clears her throat.
Phoebe plays the guitar and sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat what are they feeding you?" Through a glass window behind her, three backup singers approach microphones and begin to sing along.
The three backup singers continue to vocalize loudly, their voices overlapping with Phoebe's guitar playing.
Phoebe stops playing the guitar. Her eyes widen as she says, "Oh, my God."
Phoebe turns her head slightly, keeping her gaze directed toward the glass window.
Inside a recording studio control room, a woman and a man sit behind a mixing board, looking through a glass window. The woman smiles.
Off-screen, Phoebe asks, "Who was that?" The woman chuckles.
The woman leans closer to the microphone on the desk.
The woman says, "They're your backup singers."
The woman continues, "Behind you, hon."
In the recording booth, Phoebe turns around, looking through the glass at the three singers standing behind her. She gasps and waves one hand.
The singers wave back. Phoebe says, "I thought they were just watching me,"
Phoebe turns slightly and points back toward the singers.
Phoebe turns her head slightly and laughs, saying, "like at an aquarium."
Phoebe continues laughing and lowers her hand.
Looking back toward the singers, Phoebe says, "Wow, they're so good. Hi."
Phoebe says, "You're so good. All right." The singers wave and smile.
Now in the control room, the woman touches the mixing board.
The woman speaks into the microphone, "Okay, now that everyone knows everyone, let's try it again. Okay, from the top."
In the booth, Phoebe plays the acoustic guitar and sings, "Smelly cat, smelly cat what are they feeding you?" The backup singers sway side to side.
The backup singers sing loudly, "Smelly cat, smelly really bad smelly, smelly cat." Phoebe continues playing the guitar.
Phoebe abruptly stops playing the guitar and raises her hands.
Phoebe waves her hands slightly and says, "okay, sorry. I'm just..."
Phoebe places one hand on her chest and gestures outward, adding, "...not getting that everyone gets how smelly this"
In the control room, the woman and the man look toward the glass as Phoebe says off-screen, "cat actually is. I just think that maybe if we could talk about this..."
Now visible in the booth, Phoebe gestures with her hands as she says, "...because I need to feel that you really care about the"
Phoebe finishes, "cat."
The backup singers look toward Phoebe. In the control room, the woman leans forward.
The woman speaks into the microphone, "Honey, we can talk about this. It's just that it's costing about $100 a minute..."
Now visible in the booth, Phoebe nods. The woman continues off-screen, "...to be in here." Phoebe says, "Oh, okay. So the cat"
Phoebe adjusts her grip on the guitar neck and says, "stinks, but you love it. Let's go." She begins strumming the guitar.
The setting changes to a living room. Ross walks into the room, holding a phone to his ear.
Ross paces as he says, "No, there was no way he was a velociraptor. No, Tony, look at the cranial ridge, okay?"
Ross continues, "If Dina was a velociraptor, he would have eaten the"
Ross continues speaking into the phone and turns around. Monica enters the living room from the hallway, wearing a red robe.
Ross lowers the phone slightly and asks, "oh, were you taking a nap?" Monica stops walking and replies, "I was."
Ross raises the phone back to his ear, saying, "Oh, wait, Tony, can you hold on? That's the other line." He presses a button on the phone and says, "Hello?"
Ross says, "Oh, yeah, she's here, but can she call you back?" Monica stands in the background, her arms slightly raised.
Ross holds the phone to his ear, keeping his gaze steady.
Ross says, "Okay, thanks," and presses a button. Looking at Monica, he adds, "Call Joanna."
Monica asks, "Hi. Did she leave a number?" Ross replies, "Did you see me write one down?"
Monica drops her arms to her sides and stares at Ross.
Monica says, "I don't have her number, buttmunch."
Ross pulls the phone slightly away from his chest and looks back at Monica.
Ross says, "Well, she'll call back. Don't be such a baby." Monica retorts, "I'm not a baby."
Ross points at Monica and says, "You're the baby." Monica steps closer, raising her hands, and says, "Look, you want to get off my back? You want to get out of my face?"
Ross raises the phone to his ear and says, "Wait. Hold on. Tony. Hold on." He presses a button and says, "Hello?"
Ross holds the phone to his ear, waiting.
Ross says, "Hi."
Ross nods slightly, listening to the caller.
Ross says, "Yeah, no, she's right here. Hold on," and presses a button on the phone.
Speaking into the phone again, Ross says, "Hi, Tony. Can I call you back?" He lowers the phone, looking at Monica, and adds, "That's my"
Ross finishes, "sister's boyfriend." Monica reaches for the phone, saying, "Give me that." Ross replies, "Okay," and hands it to her.
Monica takes the phone, lightly taps her forehead with the antenna, and walks toward the kitchen area. Ross turns away.
Monica holds the phone to her ear and says, "Hi, sweetie. Listen, before I forget, did I leave my diaphragm at your place?"
Monica pauses, holding the phone to her ear. Ross, standing nearby, turns his head sharply toward her.
Monica's expression drops, and she says, "Hi, mom."
Ross laughs loudly, pointing at Monica, and quickly walks toward the hallway. Monica turns and swings her arm toward him in frustration. Ross exits the room.

Monica stands alone in the kitchen, keeping the phone to her ear. Instrumental transition music begins as the setting changes to an exterior daytime view of a city skyline, followed by a brick apartment building.
The instrumental music stops. Inside an apartment kitchen, Chandler sits on the counter, tossing a small yellow ball in his hand. He drops the ball, and it bounces onto the floor.
Chandler briefly raises his hands. The front door opens, and Joey walks in. Chandler turns toward Joey and makes a finger-gun gesture.
Joey steps further into the apartment, holding a small item. He tosses it lightly in his hand and looks toward Chandler.

In the apartment kitchen, Chandler sits on the counter holding a small yellow ball, wearing a maroon long-sleeve shirt. Joey, wearing a black leather jacket, stands near the doorway. Chandler asks, "So, how's the palace?" Joey gestures with his hands and replies, "You know, it's funny you should mention that..."
Joey continues, "...because I was thinking," and stops speaking. He lowers his hands and looks past Chandler toward cardboard boxes stacked in the adjacent living room.
Joey points toward the boxes and asks, "what's with the..." Chandler slides off the counter, still holding the ball, and says, "oh, uh, actually, I have some news." Audience laughter is heard.
Eddie walks into the living room from a hallway, wearing a plaid shirt and carrying a foam moose hat with large yellow antlers. He says, "Hey,"
Eddie continues, "Chan, is that Joey guy going to come by and pick up his moose hat, or should I just toss it out?" while holding up the hat. Joey turns his head to look at him.
Chandler gestures toward Joey with the ball and says, "Well, why don't you ask him yourself? Joey, this is my new roommate, Eddie."
Eddie looks at Joey and says, "Nice to meet you." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey keeps his gaze on Eddie and says, "Likewise. I know."
Joey steps toward Eddie, takes the moose hat from his hands, and says, "I'll take that as what I came for." Joey holds the hat at his side. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey gestures with the hat toward Chandler and says, "So, this is what's new."
Joey looks back at Eddie and asks, "Where'd you two meet?"
Eddie points toward Chandler and says, "At the supermarket in the ethnic food section." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler smiles and says, "I helped him pick out a chorizo." Joey looks at Chandler, his facial expression remaining unchanged. Audience laughter continues.
Joey lowers his eyes and says, "Wow." Chandler gestures and says, "We got to talking, and he said he needed a place, and I had a spare room." Joey softly says, "Oh."
Joey stands silently, looking slightly downward, while Chandler keeps his eyes on him. The scene fades to black.
The screen remains black, and silence continues until the end of the segment.
